output_freshness.decider: 3.2 hours ago (09:45 local) — within daily schedule
output_freshness.integrity: 1 hour ago (12:03 local) — within daily schedule

handoff_issues.description: code_analysis_tags produced in 59 curmudgeon review JSONs but never applied to wins.json. Integrity correctly reports 0/67 code_analysis reviewed in wins.json. The decider's morning briefing says 'Apply code_analysis tags' as a NEXT SESSION action but produces no automated patches for this. 59 WINs worth of tag data is accumulating with no pipeline to ingest it.
handoff_issues.evidence: monitor/integrity/report-2026-04-06.json shows code_analysis.reviewed=0, pending=67. monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ contains 59 review files, all with code_analysis_tags populated. monitor/decisions/suggested-patches.json contains 0 patches targeting the code_analysis field. Morning briefing mentions 'Apply code_analysis tags for WIN-035 through WIN-041' but this has been accumulating since WIN-001 was reviewed on April 5.

suggested_fix.rationale: The decider prompt should instruct the decider to generate suggested patches for wins.json code_analysis fields based on curmudgeon review JSONs. Alternatively, a script could be written to batch-apply tags from reviews/ to wins.json. This is a design gap, not a simple text fix — flagging for human review.

handoff_issues.description: Decider runs at ~06:30 UTC daily; integrity runs at ~09:00 UTC daily. The decider always consumes yesterday's integrity report, missing same-day integrity findings by 2.5 hours. In practice this hasn't caused problems because issues found by integrity are typically fixed in the human session and confirmed on the next cycle.
handoff_issues.evidence: daily-report-2026-04-07.json (generated_at 2026-04-07T06:30:00Z) references integrity issues from report-2026-04-06.json (run_at 2026-04-06T11:03:24Z). The decider correctly notes all were fixed, but it's always operating on stale integrity data.

suggested_fix.rationale: Schedule reorder would fix this (integrity before decider) but schedule changes need human approval. Current impact is low since the daily cycle self-corrects. Flag for consideration only.

handoff_issues.description: Integrity report found 5 broken external links (CHAMP, GRACE, Apollo LRR, CUORE SSL, CHIME DNS). ISS-032 addressed 3 of these (CHAMP, GRACE, CHIME) but 2 were not captured as issues: Apollo 11 LRR (LPI site restructured) and CUORE (expired SSL certificate). These orphaned findings have no open issue tracking them.
handoff_issues.evidence: integrity report-2026-04-06.json lists 5 broken links. open-issues.json ISS-032 only covers 3. No ISS entry exists for LPI Apollo 11 LRR (404) or CUORE (expired SSL).

suggested_fix.rationale: Decider should create ISS entries for these on next run. If they persist, flag for human attention. The CUORE SSL issue may self-resolve; the Apollo LRR URL needs updating to current NASA/ILRS site.

prompt_issues.description: Config only lists 3 of 6 agents (poller, analyst, decider). Curmudgeon, integrity, and tinker are absent. Any agent or process that reads config.json to understand the pipeline will have an incomplete picture.

suggested_fix.rationale: Adds missing agents to config. Also fixes decider schedule from 'every 12 hours' to 'daily 6:30 AM UTC' to match actual behavior documented in CLAUDE.md.

prompt_issues.description: Tinker prompt says curmudgeon runs 'every 10min' but CLAUDE.md says 'every 15min'. Observed output rate (~4 reviews/hour over 14 hours) is more consistent with 15-minute intervals.

suggested_fix.rationale: Aligns tinker prompt with CLAUDE.md and observed curmudgeon output rate.

missing_patches.description: Decider says 'requires reading full WIN-016 detail to locate incomplete sentence' — the decider has read access to wins.json and could read the detail text. This is a lazy deferral if the decider isn't attempting to read the file.
Decider says 'requires deeper analysis of dome's specific symmetry claim' — reasonable for now but the decider should attempt this analysis rather than indefinitely deferring.

open_issue_health.notes: All 14 open issues were created today/yesterday. No issues aged >7 days yet. Most deferrals have reasonable rationales (manual verification, cross-issue coordination). Two issues (ISS-015, ISS-020) have deferral rationales that describe tasks the decider could attempt. Monitor on next cycle.

schedule_issues.description: Curmudgeon has produced 59 review files in ~38 hours (Apr 5 22:22 to Apr 6 12:37). That's ~1.55 reviews/hour or ~1 review per 39 minutes. If the curmudgeon runs every 15 minutes, only ~40% of runs produce new reviews. This is expected since reviews take variable time, but it means Phase 1 completion for WINs (8 remaining) will take approximately 5 more hours at current rate, not the 1.7 days estimated in the morning briefing.
schedule_issues.recommendation: Morning briefing's '1.7 days' estimate appears too pessimistic based on current throughput. 59/67 reviewed in ~38 hours = ~0.6 hours per WIN = ~5 hours for remaining 8 WINs. After WINs: 24 sections + 3 prose + 9 holistic = 36 items remain for Phase 1 completion.

cross_check_findings.finding: WIN-059 review is thorough and well-structured. code_analysis_tags correctly identify monitoring='none', relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false — all with detailed evidence strings. The review correctly identifies that dome's Kill-Shot Test 6 is currently FAILING at 39.9% error, which is devastating and missing from our published text. Two holes found (duplicate mislabeling, missing Test 6 failure evidence) are substantive and actionable.

Morning briefing accurately summarizes pipeline state. Suggested patches are well-formed with exact find/replace text. However, the curmudgeon progress count (41/67) was stale at time of reporting — the tracker showed 59/67 when the decider ran (based on file timestamps). The decider may have cached an earlier tracker read or the tracker was updated between runs.
cross_check_findings.quality: good — stale count is a timing artifact, not a logic error

cross_check_findings.finding: Report correctly identifies all broken anchors and external links. Build reproducibility check is valuable. However, the code_analysis count (0/67 reviewed) reflects wins.json state, not curmudgeon progress — this is technically correct but may mislead readers into thinking the curmudgeon hasn't reviewed any WINs. The integrity report should note the distinction.
cross_check_findings.quality: good — accurate but could add context about curmudgeon review progress vs wins.json state

recommendations.description: Create a script or decider instruction to batch-apply code_analysis_tags from curmudgeon review JSONs to wins.json. 59 WINs have been reviewed but wins.json shows 0/67 — the most valuable curmudgeon output is not reaching the published site.
Update config.json to include all 6 agents with correct schedules. Currently missing curmudgeon, integrity, tinker; decider schedule incorrect.
Fix curmudgeon schedule in tinker.md from 'Every 10min' to 'Every 15min' to match CLAUDE.md and observed behavior.
Decider should create ISS entries for Apollo LRR (404) and CUORE (expired SSL) broken links found by integrity but not yet tracked.
Consider reordering integrity (currently 9 AM) to run before decider (currently 6:30 AM), so the decider gets same-day integrity data. Low impact currently.
